daily_brief: The day's headlines are dominated by volatile developments in the Iran conflict, with President Trump announcing he has called off a planned major assault on Iran at the request of Gulf states, citing ongoing negotiations [BBC, Globe and Mail]. This comes as his approval rating has dropped to 37%, with 64% of Americans now opposing the war [BBC]. In public health, the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has escalated sharply, with at least 131 deaths and over 500 suspected cases prompting the WHO to declare an international emergency and the Africa CDC to declare a continental emergency [BBC, France 24, RFI, Guardian]. The outbreak has spread to Uganda and reached the city of Goma, with experts warning the true scale remains unknown. On the diplomatic front, Russian President Putin arrived in Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping, seeking to demonstrate the strength of the Russia-China partnership just days after Trump's high-profile visit to China produced trade headlines but no breakthrough on Ukraine [France 24, RFI, BBC]. In the United States, a suspected hate crime at a San Diego mosque left three dead after two teenage gunmen opened fire before taking their own lives [BBC, Al Jazeera, Globe and Mail]. Meanwhile, Japan and South Korea agreed to strengthen energy cooperation amid Iran war disruptions at a summit in Andong [Nikkei Asia, The Hindu].

top_stories.headline: Trump Calls Off Planned Iran Attack, Citing Gulf State Request and Ongoing Negotiations
bullets: President Trump announced on Truth Social that he has paused a 'large scale assault' on Iran planned for Tuesday at the request of Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE leaders, who indicated a deal would be reached
Iran submitted a new peace proposal to Washington; Iranian Foreign Ministry confirmed their views had been conveyed to the US
Trump warned the US remains ready to attack 'on a moment's notice' if no acceptable deal is reached, demanding 'NO NUCLEAR WEAPONS FOR IRAN'
A New York Times/Siena poll shows 64% of voters believe the war was wrong and Trump's approval has fallen to 37%

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes domestic political pressure on Trump, leads with polling showing war unpopularity

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focuses on negotiation mechanics and Strait of Hormuz pressure, more neutral diplomatic framing

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provides regional context including UAE economic damage from the war

top_stories.headline: DRC Ebola Outbreak Kills at Least 131 as WHO and Africa CDC Declare Emergencies
bullets: At least 131 deaths reported from 513 suspected cases of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola in Democratic Republic of Congo
WHO declared a 'public health emergency of international concern'; Africa CDC declared a Continental Public Health Emergency
Outbreak has spread beyond initial area to Goma, Butembo, and into Uganda (2 confirmed cases, 1 death)
No approved vaccine or specific treatment exists for this strain; an American doctor working in DRC has tested positive

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive coverage including American doctor case and WHO response

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes rising death toll and emergency meeting preparations

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Features interview with Ebola co-discoverer warning authorities may have detected outbreak too late

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Focuses on ground-level panic and economic impacts in affected communities

top_stories.headline: Putin Arrives in Beijing to Reinforce China Ties Days After Trump's Visit
bullets: Russian President Putin began a two-day state visit to China for talks with Xi Jinping, marking his annual visit since the 2022 Ukraine invasion
Visit coincides with 25th anniversary of China-Russia strategic partnership treaty and comes just four days after Trump left Beijing
Kremlin stated leaders will discuss strengthening comprehensive partnership and sign a joint declaration
Russia remains diplomatically isolated and economically dependent on China as the main buyer of sanctioned Russian oil

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Notes the 'unshakeable' ties narrative Putin seeks to project

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes that Trump's visit produced no breakthrough on Ukraine

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Provides analysis of the 'misunderstood partnership' and power dynamics between leaders

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Headline emphasizes 'deeply unbalanced relations' favoring China

top_stories.headline: Three Killed in Suspected Hate Crime Shooting at San Diego Mosque
bullets: Two teenage gunmen (ages 17 and 18) fatally shot three men at the Islamic Center of San Diego before killing themselves blocks away
Attack occurred two hours after one suspect's mother called police reporting her son had run away with weapons and was possibly suicidal
A security guard among the victims 'played a pivotal role' in preventing worse casualties; police called his actions 'heroic'
Investigators found 'hate rhetoric' in writings attributed to one suspect; attack being investigated as a hate crime

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Straightforward factual account emphasizing security guard's heroism

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Provides context of attack occurring before Eid al-Adha holiday

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Details police timeline and mother's warning call

top_stories.headline: Japan and South Korea Strengthen Energy Cooperation Amid Iran War Disruptions
bullets: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and South Korean President Lee Jae Myung held their fourth meeting in six months at a summit in Andong, South Korea
Leaders agreed to strengthen supply chain cooperation to mitigate energy disruptions caused by the US-Israeli war with Iran
Meeting is part of 'hometown summits' series marking first time sitting leaders have visited each other's hometowns
Summit agenda includes economic cooperation, Iran war impacts, and bilateral ties development

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Leads with energy supply chain cooperation as primary outcome

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes geopolitical context and positive trajectory of relationship

additional_stories.headline: Russia begins three-day nuclear weapons drills involving 65,000 troops as Putin visits China
US suspends joint defence advisory board with Canada, citing failure to meet military commitments
EU-China SMILE spacecraft launches successfully from French Guiana on solar exploration mission
Gunmen abduct 39 students and 7 teachers in coordinated attacks on three schools in Nigeria's Oyo State
Libyan militia commander accused of torture of refugees to appear at ICC in landmark hearing
Australia scales back planned upgrades to Collins Class submarines, will not replace diesel engines
Undersea volcano erupts in Papua New Guinea's Bismarck Sea, prompting tsunami warnings
Louvre selects architects for €800 million renovation including new dedicated space for Mona Lisa
Los Angeles World Cup stadium workers threaten strike if ICE agents deployed during matches
Shakira wins €55 million tax refund from Spain after court rules she was not a resident in 2011
